title: Terraform Modules Quiz
description: Test your knowledge on grouping resources, child modules, and the registry.

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of using Terraform Modules?

options.text: It eliminates the need to pay for AWS services.
It packages complex infrastructure setups into reusable, parameterised blocks, reducing code duplication.
It is exactly replacing Docker containers.
It speeds up the Terraform binary execution time.

questions.question: What exactly is a Terraform 'Module' at a basic level?

options.text: A specialized ZIP file downloaded from HashiCorp.
A virtual machine pre-configured with Terraform.
Any directory containing Terraform configuration (.tf) files.
A Docker image containing the state file.

questions.question: When defining a module block linking to a public registry component, what is a crucial best practice?

options.text: Always run 'terraform init' with the --force flag.
Explicitly define a specific 'version' argument to prevent unexpected breaking changes from auto-updating.
Never pass input variables; rely on the registry's defaults.
Always keep the source argument blank.

questions.question: Which command downloads the code for the modules specified in your configuration?

options.text: terraform module download
terraform apply
terraform init
terraform refresh

questions.question: Inside a typical module folder, which file is standardly used to define the input parameters expected by the module?

options.text: inputs.tf
variables.tf
main.tf
parameters.JSON
